summary: The root element of every HTML document; all other elements descend from it.
use: Exactly once per document, carrying lang and dir so assistive technology and search engines know the document's language and direction.
avoid: Never omit lang — screen readers will guess the pronunciation engine and often guess wrong.
attributes: lang — BCP 47 language tag (e.g. en, pt-BR)

summary: The container for machine-readable metadata: title, charset, viewport, links, and structured data.
use: Once per document; put charset within the first 1024 bytes and the title early.
avoid: Don't put renderable content here — browsers will relocate it into body and your document becomes invalid.

summary: The document's name, shown in the browser tab, bookmarks, and search results.
use: One per document, unique across your site, ~50–60 characters, most important words first.
avoid: Don't stuff keywords or reuse one title site-wide — it is the single strongest on-page SEO signal.

summary: Sets the base URL for every relative URL in the document.
use: Almost never — only when a document is deliberately served from multiple locations and must resolve assets against one canonical base.
avoid: It rewrites fragment links too (#main skip links, SVG url(#id) references), silently breaking them; prefer correct relative paths.
attributes: target — default browsing context for links

summary: Connects the document to external resources: canonical URLs, stylesheets, icons, feeds, hreflang alternates, and resource hints.
use: For rel=canonical on every page, rel=alternate hreflang for translations, rel=icon, and preconnect/preload performance hints.
avoid: Don't preload what you don't use — unused hints waste bandwidth and get flagged by devtools.
attributes: rel — relationship (canonical, stylesheet, icon, alternate, preconnect…)

summary: Metadata that no other element expresses: charset, viewport, description, Open Graph, robots directives.
use: charset first in head, viewport for responsive rendering, one unique description (~150–160 chars) per page.
avoid: Skip meta keywords (ignored since 2009) and only add robots when you need to restrict indexing.
snippet: <meta name='description' content='One compelling sentence describing this page.'>

summary: Embeds CSS directly in the document head.
use: For small critical CSS inlined for performance, or single-file self-contained documents.
avoid: For anything beyond trivial size, an external stylesheet caches independently of the HTML; and never use style as a substitute for semantic element choice.

summary: The sectioning root holding all renderable content.
use: Once per document; keep all perceivable content inside landmark elements within it, with a skip link first.
avoid: Don't scatter loose content directly in body — screen-reader users navigate by landmarks, and orphaned content is invisible to that navigation.
